    • How to test ?
        • Button
        • Link
        • Heading
        • Image
        • Table
        • List
        • Playground
        • Label
        • Input
        • Textarea
        • Content Editable
        • Checkbox
        • Radio Group
        • Switch

        • aria-labelledby
        • aria-describedby
        • aria-expanded
        • aria-current
        • aria-live
        • Modal
        • Accordion
        • Tabs

      Radio Group

      Radio buttons allow users to select a single option from a group.

      Unlike checkboxes, only one radio button within a group can be selected at a time.

      Radio buttons should almost always be grouped using a fieldset and legend.

      Example

      Preferred Contact Method
      export function RadioGroup() {
  return (
    <fieldset>
      <legend>Preferred Contact Method</legend>

      <div>
        <input id="contact-email" type="radio" name="contact" />

        <label htmlFor="contact-email">Email</label>
      </div>

      <div>
        <input id="contact-phone" type="radio" name="contact" />

        <label htmlFor="contact-phone">Phone</label>
      </div>

      <div>
        <input id="contact-sms" type="radio" name="contact" />

        <label htmlFor="contact-sms">SMS</label>
      </div>
    </fieldset>
  );
}

      Accessibility Notes

      • Use the same name attribute for related radios.
      • Group radios using fieldset.
      • Provide a group label using legend.
      • Every radio button should have an associated label.

      Basic Radio Group

      <fieldset>
  <legend>Preferred Contact Method</legend>

  <div>
    <input id="contact-email" type="radio" name="contact" />

    <label for="contact-email"> Email </label>
  </div>

  <div>
    <input id="contact-phone" type="radio" name="contact" />

    <label for="contact-phone"> Phone </label>
  </div>

  <div>
    <input id="contact-sms" type="radio" name="contact" />

    <label for="contact-sms"> SMS </label>
  </div>
</fieldset>

      Screen reader:

      Preferred Contact Method
group

Email
radio button
1 of 3

      Required Radio Group

      <fieldset>
  <legend>Preferred Contact Method</legend>

  <input type="radio" name="contact" required />

  ...
</fieldset>

      Screen reader:

      Preferred Contact Method
required
group

      Radio Group with Description

      <fieldset aria-describedby="contact-help">
  <legend>Preferred Contact Method</legend>

  ...
</fieldset>

<p id="contact-help">Select the method we should use to contact you.</p>

      Screen reader:

      Preferred Contact Method
group

Select the method we should use to contact you.

      Radio Group with Error

      <fieldset aria-describedby="contact-error">
  <legend>Preferred Contact Method</legend>

  ...
</fieldset>

<p id="contact-error">Please select a contact method.</p>

      Screen reader:

      Preferred Contact Method
group

Please select a contact method.

      Keyboard Support

      Native radio groups provide built-in keyboard support.

      KeyAction
      TabEnter radio group
      Shift + TabLeave radio group
      Arrow UpPrevious option
      Arrow DownNext option
      Arrow LeftPrevious option
      Arrow RightNext option
      SpaceSelect focused radio

      Why Fieldset and Legend Matter

      Without grouping:

      <input type="radio" name="contact" />

<input type="radio" name="contact" />

<input type="radio" name="contact" />

      Screen reader users hear:

      Email radio button
Phone radio button
SMS radio button

      but may not know what question these options answer.

      With a fieldset:

      <fieldset>
  <legend>Preferred Contact Method</legend>

  ...
</fieldset>

      Screen reader users hear:

      Preferred Contact Method
group

      before entering the radio buttons.

      Common Mistakes

      ❌ Missing Labels

      <input type="radio" />

      Screen readers cannot determine what the option represents.

      ✅ Associated Labels

      <input id="email" type="radio" />

<label for="email"> Email </label>

      ❌ Different Name Attributes

      <input type="radio" name="email" />

<input type="radio" name="phone" />

      These become separate radio groups.

      Users can select both options.

      ✅ Shared Name Attribute

      <input type="radio" name="contact" />

<input type="radio" name="contact" />

      Only one option can be selected.

      ❌ No Fieldset

      <input type="radio" name="contact" />

<input type="radio" name="contact" />

      The relationship between options is unclear.

      ✅ Use Fieldset and Legend

      <fieldset>
  <legend>Preferred Contact Method</legend>

  ...
</fieldset>

      Radio vs Checkbox

      Radio

      <input type="radio" />

      Use when:

      Only one option may be selected.

      Examples:

      • Payment method
      • Shipping method
      • Contact method

      Checkbox

      <input type="checkbox" />

      Use when:

      Multiple options may be selected.

      Examples:

      • Notification preferences
      • Interests
      • Hobbies

      When to Use

      Use radio buttons when:

      • Users must choose exactly one option.
      • All options should remain visible.
      • The number of options is relatively small.

      Examples:

      • Payment Method
      • Contact Preference
      • Theme Selection
      • Shipping Option

      For larger lists, consider a Select instead.
